pattern: Thread start order is meaningless — only explicit signaling (an event or semaphore released by the previous stage) can impose an execution order across threads.
hints: Sleeping and hoping the scheduler cooperates is not synchronization — the judge starts the threads in every possible order.
Each stage should wait on a gate that only the previous stage opens.
Two events (or two zero-initialized semaphores) suffice: first() opens the gate for second(), second() opens the gate for third().

edge_inputs.description: threads started in the natural order
first starts, then third, then second
